revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. V..."
revision.text:
'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|ja|ギリシア神話}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|ja|プローテウス}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in {{ill|Shiogama|ja|塩竈市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}’s account of the {{ill|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|ja|天孫降臨}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi|ja|ニニギ}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}.
During the {{ill|Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武東征}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|ja|六所神社 (岡崎市)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine|ja|塩屋神社}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takamu Shrine'''|高牟神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].
